data.body:
CORRECTED FIX DIRECTION (per review): the right behavior is NOT to refuse reconcile under readonly, nor a bespoke MCP path — it's to make cap-0 modes honor the contract the Mode docstring ALREADY states:
    RECONCILE_CHECK -- read-only discrepancy report; no writes
    DRY_RUN         -- read-only analysis; no Jira or ticket writes
reconcile_once already RETURNS the computed mutation plan (the report). The bug is that the persistence side-effects run regardless of mode. So: keep the report, skip ALL local writes when MODE_CAPS[mode]==0.

Write points to gate on a writing-mode (cap!=0) / persist flag in reconcile_once (verified by code read):
  1. SyncLogger init+log+close -> bridge_state/sync-log-<pass>.jsonl   (use a no-op logger when not persisting)
  2. fetcher.fetch_snapshot -> bridge_state/snapshots/<pass>.json      (needs a compute-without-persist path returning the dict)
  3. invariants.check_at_most_one_local_id / check_dual_identity_complete / report_schema_drift -> file local bug tickets (CREATE events) — skip
  4. health.record_pass -> bridge_state/health/<ts>.json               — skip
  5. applier pass manifest -> bridge_state/snapshots/<pass>.manifest.json — skip
  6. binding_store.save() -> .tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/bindings.json (+ prev_snapshot.json) — skip
prev_snapshot is READ-only (tracker/.bridge_state), so the report stays accurate without persisting curr.

Entanglement: the returned report's mutations_applied is currently read back from the persisted manifest (reconcile.py ~:1150) — a no-persist mode must source the report from the in-memory  list instead.

SCOPE QUESTION: the docstring says dry-run/reconcile-check are no-write PERIOD, so the most-correct fix makes cap-0 no-write EVERYWHERE (CLI + library + MCP), not just under REBAR_MCP_READONLY. That also changes CLI  (currently persists a snapshot). Confirm universal vs MCP-readonly-only before implementing.

Blast radius: reconcile core + 5 helper modules + existing tests that reference dry-run/reconcile-check (test_reconcile_check_bindings_dict.py, test_mode*, test_sync_logger.py, etc.) — do as a dedicated change with full reconciler-suite verification.
